title: 2. Defining strict Pydantic structures for tool inputs

blocks.content: Pydantic offers strict, runtime class validation. By using Pydantic models in tool signatures, you ensure parameters adhere to specific constraints (like string length, email format, or enum constants).

blocks.title: Validation Failures
blocks.content: If the LLM passes an invalid payload (e.g. invalid email structure), Pydantic triggers a ValidationError, which ADK returns to the LLM to auto-correct.

Question 1: What happens when a tool argument fails Pydantic validation?
- A) The host program crashes.
- B) The agent exits immediately.
- C) A validation exception returns to the LLM so it can attempt a corrected tool call.

Feedback: Option C is correct. ADK catches validation errors and allows the agent loop to self-heal.
